name: Vía Verde del Urola – round trip from Azcoitia
name_original: Vía Verde del Urola – ida y vuelta desde Azcoitia
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Azkoitia traverse a diverse landscape within Spain's Basque Country, characterized by valleys, mountains, and lush green countryside. The region is protected by the Izarraitz massif, providing a mountainous backdrop for many routes. Terrain varies from well-paved surfaces to unpaved segments, including notable features like the Urola Greenway and the Elosua climb. Best touring cycling routes around Azkoitia The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Verde del Urola – round trip from Azcoitia, a 18.5 miles (29.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route follows a notable "green road" featuring numerous bridges and tunnels, offering an accessible and scenic cycling experience. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Azkoitia – Hermitage of Santa Bárbara loop from Azkoitia, a difficult 31.5 miles (50.7 km) path. This route offers a mountainous backdrop and traverses forested areas, providing varied scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Urola Greenway – Urola Greenway loop from Loiola, a 20.6 miles (33.2 km) trail leading through lush green countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Touring cycling around Azkoitia is defined by the Izarraitz massif, the Urola Greenway, and the Elosua climb. The network offers options for different ability levels, from accessible greenways to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Azkoitia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Azkoitia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Azkoitia?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Azkoitia, offering a diverse range of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes more than 600 times.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Azkoitia?
A: Routes around Azkoitia feature a varied terrain, from well-paved surfaces to unpaved segments. You'll encounter lush green countryside, mountainous backdrops from the Izarraitz massif, and notable features like the Urola Greenway, which is generally accessible, and the challenging Elosua climb.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Azkoitia?
A: Yes, for an accessible and scenic experience suitable for families, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079121032/via-verde-del-urola-round-trip-from-azcoitia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Urola – round trip from Azcoitia&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This "green road" features numerous bridges and tunnels, offering a pleasant ride for all ages. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38277004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urola Greenway – Urola Greenway loop from Loiola&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What cultural and historical landmarks can I see along touring cycling routes near Azkoitia?
A: Azkoitia and its surroundings are rich in cultural heritage. You can explore the town's historical architecture, including the Gaztan Enea Palace and the Church of Santa María la Real. The nearby Sanctuary of Loyola, birthplace of St. Ignatius, is also accessible via routes like the Urola Greenway. While specific routes aren't designed solely for cultural stops, many pass through or near these points of interest.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10524811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azkoitia – Hermitage of Santa Bárbara loop from Azkoitia&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain, traversing mountainous and forested areas. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31534110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azkoitia – Lastur loop from Azkoitia&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1100 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features or scenic viewpoints can I discover while cycling around Azkoitia?
A: The region offers stunning natural beauty. You can cycle through lush green countryside with the Izarraitz massif as a backdrop. While inland, the dramatic Basque coastline with its unique geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algorri Flysch&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1101604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zumaia Flysch Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; are within reach. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass by viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3562432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendatagaina Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Azkoitia?
A: The Basque Country generally offers pleasant cycling conditions from spring through autumn. The lush green landscapes are vibrant during these seasons. While specific weather conditions can vary, avoiding the peak heat of summer or the colder, wetter winter months typically provides the most enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Azkoitia?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Azkoitia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079121032/via-verde-del-urola-round-trip-from-azcoitia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Urola – round trip from Azcoitia&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15011400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urola Greenway – Azkoitia loop from Azkoitia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Azkoitia?
A: The touring cycling routes in Azkoitia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from accessible greenways to challenging mountain ascents, and the opportunity to experience the region's natural beauty and cultural sites.

Q: Are there cafes or restaurants along the Urola Greenway for a break?
A: The Urola Greenway, being a popular route, often has amenities available in the towns and villages it passes through. While specific cafes aren't listed, you can typically find places to stop for refreshments and meals in Azkoitia, Loiola, and other settlements along the route.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists in the Azkoitia region?
A: The Basque Country has a public transport network, and some services may accommodate bicycles. It's advisable to check with local bus or train operators in advance regarding their policies for transporting bikes, especially if you plan to combine cycling with public transport to access different parts of the region.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Azkoitia?
A: Azkoitia, as a town, offers various parking options. For routes starting directly from Azkoitia, you can usually find public parking facilities within or on the outskirts of the town. For routes like those on the Urola Greenway, designated parking areas are often available near trailheads in towns like Azkoitia or Loiola.
tips: Azcoitia is a quiet and pleasant destination, surrounded by a beautiful natural landscape and perfect for enjoying local cuisine and traditions.
Nice cycle track following the route of an old train track, offering a quiet and accessible route through a beautiful rural landscape. A perfect plan to enjoy nature with the family.

Beautiful town nestled in the Guipuzcoan valley of Alto Urola. The name of the town comes from the Basque and means 'place of the elm'. The river Urola runs through the town and separates it from the municipality of Urretxu.
Zumarraga is also the end point of the Vía Verde del Urola, a cycling route that runs through a beautiful rural landscape. A destination with a lot of charm and personality.

waypoints.name: Urola Greenway Between Azkoitia and Zumarraga
